findings.title: QueryDSL repository deviates from `Custom*Repository` naming convention
findings.suggested_fix: Rename to `CustomSeasonInsightRepository` to match every other JPAQueryFactory-based repo in the codebase: `CustomOrderRepository`, `CustomOrderSessionRepository`, `CustomProductRepository`. The `Custom*Repository` prefix is a 100% consistent convention across the repo for QueryDSL-backed repositories. The bare `*Repository` name is reserved for Spring Data JPA interfaces (e.g., `OrderRepository`, `WorkspaceRepository`). A reader scanning the package will mistake this for a JPA interface.

findings.title: Constructor parameter names use abbreviations instead of full repository/service names
findings.suggested_fix: Existing services consistently inject collaborators using full bean names (`OrderService.kt`: `orderRepository`, `customOrderRepository`, `orderRedisRepository`; `StatisticsCalculator.kt`: `dailyOrderStatisticRepository`, `workspaceRepository`). Rename `insightRepo` → `seasonInsightRepository` (or `customSeasonInsightRepository` after the rename above), `metricsCalc` → `seasonMetricsCalculator`, `tierResolver` → `milestoneTierResolver`. Same applies to `SeasonDistributionService.kt:13` (`repo`, `metricsCalc`), `StrengthSelector.kt:10` (`percentileCalc`), `MilestoneTierResolver.kt:9` (`percentileCalc`), and the test file aliases. Abbreviations like `repo` are not used anywhere else in the production codebase.

findings.title: Plan doc is stale — describes WebSocket/STOMP architecture that the shipped code abandoned
findings.suggested_fix: The plan still describes the original design: STOMP push to `/sub/season-milestone/{workspaceId}` (line 5), `MilestoneWebsocketListener` (line 55, 1944), `MilestoneReachedEvent` → `@TransactionalEventListener(AFTER_COMMIT)` flow (line 7), `season_milestone_log` UNIQUE table (line 7), `dto/websocket/MilestoneReachedPayload.kt` (lines 43, 1614, 1625), `WebsocketType.MILESTONE_REACHED` (line 60), and Tasks 11-12 dedicated to STOMP publishing. The shipped code is a polling REST endpoint only — no listeners, no events, no STOMP, no log table. Either annotate the plan with a 'superseded by polling design' note at the top, or update the architecture / completed-tasks section so the plan does not mislead future readers (or anyone running ce-replay against this plan). Plans live under `docs/superpowers/plans/` which is a protected/process-artifact path, so do NOT delete the file — annotate it in place.

findings.title: DTOs and config bean named with non-domain suffix `Dto` while sibling-domain DTOs in `dto/common/` omit it
findings.suggested_fix: Existing `dto/common/` files in sibling domains (e.g., `domain/order/dto/common/OrderDto.kt`, `OrderProductDto.kt`, `OrderSessionDto.kt`) do use the `Dto` suffix, so `MilestoneStateDto`, `SocialProofDto`, `StrengthDto` are aligned. However `SeasonInsightResponse`, `SeasonDistribution`, `SeasonMetrics`, `WorkspaceSeasonAggregate` mix conventions in the same package without the suffix. Either make the package fully-suffixed (`SeasonInsightResponseDto`) or fully-unsuffixed; current state is internally inconsistent. Low-confidence because both styles exist in the wider codebase — confirm which the team prefers.

findings.title: `ClockConfiguration` bean name `systemClock` may shadow a Spring auto-config bean — convention is to name it `clock`
findings.suggested_fix: Other configurations in `global/configuration/` (e.g., `QueryDslConfiguration`) name beans by their abstract type (`jpaQueryFactory`). The bean here is named `systemClock` but is injected as `clock: Clock` everywhere. Consider renaming the `@Bean fun` to `clock()` for naming/type symmetry. Low confidence — Spring resolves by type, so this is purely stylistic.

residual_risks: No CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md exist in the repo, so review is grounded in observed patterns from sibling domains (order, statistics, workspace). If the team has unwritten conventions not visible in those domains, they were not checked.

The plan doc drift is significant enough that another agent running ce-replay or ce-validate against the plan will produce nonsense findings ('listener not implemented', 'season_milestone_log table missing'). Updating the plan or marking it superseded prevents downstream noise.
testing_gaps: No controller-layer test (`AdminSeasonInsightController`) was added; the sibling `AdminOrderStatisticsController` likewise lacks one, so this matches existing convention but does mean auth/`@AdminUsername` wiring is untested at the HTTP layer.

`SeasonInsightRepository` (QueryDSL) has no integration test against an actual database. The codebase pattern for `Custom*Repository` is also untested in many cases, so this is convention-aligned — but the `count(distinct cast({0} as date))` template and `coalesce(sum(case when ...))` expressions are non-trivial and would benefit from a `@DataJpaTest` or Testcontainers-backed test before relying on the values for donation/reward decisions.
